resources.string:
ADAS Edge is a driver-assistance aid, NOT a certified or autonomous driving system. It provides warnings only and never controls the vehicle.

Warnings may be late, inaccurate, or absent — especially where GPS is weak (tunnels, garages, dense cities), in poor light or weather, or when the camera view is obstructed. Vehicle speed is estimated from GPS, which is coarser and slower than a real car's speedometer.

You remain fully responsible for safe driving at all times. Keep your eyes on the road. Do not rely on this app to avoid a collision. Mount the device so it never obstructs your view.
Camera access is required to see the road ahead.
Location is used to estimate vehicle speed (GPS).
Records the live camera to datetime-named clips. Older clips are deleted automatically to stay within the storage cap. Clips can be retrieved for training. No recording in replay mode.
Begin recording automatically when a driving session starts. Off: recording waits until you tap the record button.
Align the lane detector to your mount: drag the line to the skyline so the sky above it is ignored.
Mount the phone as you\'ll drive, then drag the amber line onto the horizon / skyline. The dimmed area above is ignored by lane detection.

Feeds a clip instead of the live camera, with a constant synthetic speed, so warnings can be validated without driving.

Push a clip:
adb push road.mp4 /sdcard/Android/data/com.adasedge.app/files/replay.mp4

Then enable this and start driving mode.
